body.title: [sprint] The test suite WRITES INTO devlab/runtime/memory/ — and day-close then COMMITS the junk
body.description:
Found 2026-07-12 during day-close, the hard way.

THE BUG: proof_emitter / ticket-close tests emit REAL artifacts into the REAL canonical memory store. After a full-suite run, devlab/runtime/memory/ contains:
  proofs/cc.0.add-sums.T-x.*.json                      (thing='add() sums', ticket='T-x')
  proofs/cc.0.add-sums.T-proof-emitter-harness.*.json
  proofs/cc.0.add-sums.T-ticket-close-requires-proof.*.json
  proofs/cc.0.flag-removed.*.json
  sessions/cc.0.s.e.s.s.i.o.n.-.2.0.2.6.0.7.1.3.*.json  (a MANGLED namespace — 'session-2026-07-13' split character-by-character on the dotted-namespace parser, which is a SECOND bug worth its own look)

THE COMPOUNDING BUG: /day-close Step 13 says `git add devlab/runtime/memory/ docs/`. Run a suite, then day-close, and the junk gets COMMITTED. Verified: test artifacts from 2026-07-10 were ALREADY tracked in git before today, so this has been happening silently across multiple day-closes. Today's day-close committed 7 more; they have been git rm'd.

WHY IT MATTERS BEYOND TIDINESS: the memory store is the SOURCE OF TRUTH and it is grep-able-by-design. Fake proofs sitting in proofs/ are exactly the kind of thing a future session greps up and believes — a proof record for 'add() sums' against ticket 'T-x' is indistinguishable, to a grep, from a real one. This is store CORRUPTION, not clutter. (It also puts a lie in the one place the proof-on-close gate reads.)

FIX: tests must redirect the store root (the same way hermetic tests monkeypatch uu_home()) so proof_emitter/memory_emit write to tmp during tests. See CLAUDE.md: 'Tests redirect it by monkeypatching uu_home, never by setting an env var.' The store root needs the same treatment.

ALSO: consider making day-close stage explicit subpaths rather than the whole memory/ dir, so a test-junk regression cannot be silently committed again.

NOTE: two detached full-suite runs are in flight overnight (day-close audit triage) — they WILL regenerate this junk. Sweep the untracked files after they finish; do not commit them.

**Test plan:** red->green: run the suite, then assert `git status --porcelain devlab/runtime/memory/` is EMPTY. A structural guard test can assert no proof in proofs/ carries a ticket id matching the test-fixture names (T-x, T-proof-emitter-harness).

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
